question: How many residential units were launched in Bengaluru in Q1 2020?
answer: Bengaluru added approximately 8,600 new residential units in Q1 2020, a quarterly decrease of 18 per cent from Q4 2019. Over 87 per cent of the new supply was in the sub-INR 80 lakh price bracket. This was part of ANAROCK's Q1 2020 data that showed all-India launches fell 42 per cent year-on-year to approximately 41,200 units across top-7 cities.
source: ANAROCK Press Release, 26 March 2020
topic_tags: residential, launches, Q1 2020, COVID, ANAROCK

question: What was the share of total new residential launches in India that Bengaluru accounted for in Q2 2020?
answer: Bengaluru led new residential launches in Q2 2020 with approximately 49 per cent of total new launches across major Indian cities. Developers in Bengaluru pivoted aggressively to virtual tours and digital sales channels during the lockdown period. This was noted in a CREDAI Bengaluru statement and an ANAROCK consumer sentiment survey which also found that 82 per cent of Bengaluru respondents had booked homes either during or just before the lockdown.
source: CREDAI Bengaluru statement, ANAROCK Consumer Sentiment Survey 2020
topic_tags: launches, Q2 2020, COVID, CREDAI, market share

question: What monetary policy action did the RBI take in 2020 that impacted Bengaluru housing demand?
answer: The Reserve Bank of India maintained its repo rate at a record low of 4 per cent through 2020, an accommodative stance that pushed home loan rates below 7 per cent. SBI launched a repo-linked home loan at 6.95 per cent. ANAROCK noted a 40 per cent month-on-month increase in housing enquiries in Bengaluru following the rate cut cycle. The low EMI environment combined with pandemic-driven preference for home ownership created a demand tailwind that sustained through the H2 2020 festive season.
source: RBI monetary policy statements; SBI press release; ANAROCK survey
topic_tags: RBI, repo rate, home loan, affordability, SBI, monetary policy

question: What were Bengaluru's residential unit sales in Q1 2021 according to ANAROCK?
answer: Bengaluru's residential sales in Q1 2021 (January–March 2021) were approximately 8,670 units according to ANAROCK data, making it the strongest Q1 for the city since before the pandemic. At the India level, 58,290 homes were sold across the top 7 cities in Q1 2021 — breaching pre-COVID levels. Q2 2021, affected by the second COVID wave, saw Bengaluru sales fall sharply to an estimated 3,560 units, down 59 per cent quarter-on-quarter.
source: ANAROCK Q1 2021 data; Business Today 23 June 2021
topic_tags: residential, sales, Q1 2021, Q2 2021, second wave, ANAROCK

question: How many residential units were sold in Bengaluru in the full calendar year 2021 according to PropEquity?
answer: PropEquity's city-level data shows residential unit sales in Bengaluru increased to 43,181 units in the 2021 calendar year, compared with approximately 27,000-28,000 units in 2020 — representing a rise of approximately 50 per cent year-on-year. The 2021 recovery was driven by pent-up demand, record-low home loan rates and a post-second-wave rebound in H2 2021.
source: PropEquity data cited in Business Standard 28 September 2025 (historical series)
topic_tags: residential, sales, annual 2021, PropEquity, recovery

question: What tax policy changes in Union Budget 2021 were relevant to the Bengaluru real estate market?
answer: The Union Budget 2021-22 contained two significant real estate provisions: first, Section 80EEA — which offered an additional INR 1.5 lakh income tax deduction to first-time homebuyers of affordable properties on home loans sanctioned between April 2019 and March 2021 — was extended for another year to 31 March 2022. Second, TDS on REIT distributions was removed, and REITs were opened to Foreign Portfolio Investor participation, benefiting Bengaluru-anchored Embassy Office Parks REIT. The Section 80EEA extension supported demand in peripheral Bengaluru micro-markets including Electronic City, Kanakapura Road and Yelahanka where ticket sizes were still largely within the affordable threshold.
source: Finance Ministry Union Budget 2021-22; Embassy REIT investor presentations
topic_tags: budget 2021, Section 80EEA, REIT, TDS, affordable housing, Embassy REIT

question: What were Bengaluru's residential unit sales in H1 2022 and what was the year-on-year growth?
answer: Residential sales in Bengaluru surged 80 per cent year-on-year to 26,677 units in the first half of 2022 (January–June 2022), according to Knight Frank India. This compares with approximately 14,820 units sold in H1 2021. The sharp growth reflected the IT-sector employment boom, pent-up demand from the pandemic years and the release of units delayed by construction disruptions. India-wide, housing sales rose 60 per cent year-on-year in H1 2022 across eight major cities at 1,58,705 units.
source: Knight Frank India H1 2022; Brickwork Research July 2024
topic_tags: residential, sales, H1 2022, Knight Frank, YoY growth

question: What was Bengaluru's total residential unit sales for the full calendar year 2022 per PropEquity?
answer: PropEquity's city-level data shows Bengaluru residential sales reached 60,391 units in the 2022 calendar year, more than double the 2020 pandemic trough. This made 2022 the strongest residential sales year for the city in recent history at that time. The full-year performance was supported by a strong H1 2022 driven by the IT employment boom, with some moderation in H2 as RBI rate hikes began to impact affordability.
source: PropEquity data cited in Business Standard 28 September 2025 (historical series)
topic_tags: residential, sales, annual 2022, PropEquity

question: What triggered residential price increases in Bengaluru in 2022?
answer: Bengaluru residential prices rose approximately 8-15 per cent over 2022, driven by a combination of factors. First, construction material costs surged significantly — steel prices reached approximately INR 68,000 per metric tonne and cement averaged over INR 400 per bag, representing increases of 35 per cent and 28 per cent respectively from pre-pandemic levels. CREDAI Bengaluru noted that developers who had held prices flat through 2020-21 implemented phased price revisions from Q2 2022. Second, strong IT-sector demand, depleting unsold inventory and improved buyer confidence also supported price increases.
source: CREDAI Bengaluru; ANAROCK 2022; Brickwork Research
topic_tags: pricing, inflation, steel, cement, construction cost, 2022

question: What was India's Grade-A office gross absorption in 2022 and how did Bengaluru contribute?
answer: India's top-six cities recorded approximately 59.6 million square feet of gross office absorption in 2022, setting a record at that time. Bengaluru contributed approximately 33 per cent of national demand — estimated at 17-20 MSF — retaining its position as India's leading office market. The Outer Ring Road was the dominant micro-market. GCCs accounted for approximately 35 per cent of Bengaluru's absorption, with BFSI and Engineering & Manufacturing sectors growing their share of take-up.
source: JLL; Colliers; Cushman & Wakefield 2022 annual reports
topic_tags: commercial, office, absorption, 2022, Bengaluru, ORR, GCC

question: In 2023 what sector for the first time overtook Technology in Bengaluru's office leasing share?
answer: In 2023, Engineering and Manufacturing (E&M) sector occupiers captured a 26 per cent share of Bengaluru's office leasing, surpassing the Technology sector's 22 per cent share — marking the first time E&M overtook IT/Tech in Bengaluru's traditionally tech-dominated office market. This shift was noted in Colliers India's Q4 2023 press release on 24 December 2023, which observed that the IT sector's share had rationalised from over 40 per cent in earlier years, while BFSI and E&M grew significantly.
source: Colliers India Press Release 24 December 2023
topic_tags: commercial, office, E&M, technology, sector, 2023, Colliers

question: What were India's total residential unit sales in 2023 and how did Bengaluru's launch volume compare?
answer: India's top-seven cities recorded approximately 4,76,530 units of residential sales in 2023 — a 31 per cent year-on-year increase and an all-time high — according to ANAROCK. Separately, Knight Frank's top-8 city universe showed sales of 3,29,097 units in 2023 at a ten-year high. Bengaluru's residential launches grew 18 per cent in 2023 according to Knight Frank (cited in Brickwork Research), the second-highest city-wise growth rate after Kolkata. New launches across India's top-8 cities reached 3,50,746 units in 2023, also a ten-year peak.
source: ANAROCK December 2023; Knight Frank annual 2023; Brickwork Research July 2024
topic_tags: residential, sales, 2023, all-time high, India, Bengaluru, Knight Frank, ANAROCK

question: When did the Namma Metro Yellow Line become fully operational and what was its effect on property prices?
answer: The Namma Metro Yellow Line's 19-kilometre route from Rashtreeya Vidyalaya (RV) Road to Bommasandra via Electronic City became fully operational with all 16 stations in service on 10-11 August 2024. Following commissioning, property prices along the Electronic City and Kanakapura Road corridors appreciated by 8-12 per cent. NoBroker's January 2026 real estate trends article confirmed Kanakapura Road as an investment hotspot with property rates of INR 7,000-14,000 per sq ft and rental of up to INR 35,000 per month. Train frequency improved to approximately every 10 minutes during peak hours by early 2026.
source: BMRCL commissioning; NoBroker January 2026 article; Casagrand blog
topic_tags: Yellow Line, BMRCL, Namma Metro, Electronic City, Kanakapura Road, August 2024

question: What were Bengaluru's residential launch and sales volumes in 2024 and which zone dominated?
answer: Bengaluru's residential market recorded 71,000 new unit launches in 2024 — a 30 per cent year-on-year growth from 2023 — and 65,200 unit sales, a modest 2 per cent increase year-on-year, according to ANAROCK's Indian Residential Real Estate Annual Report 2024. East Bengaluru was the dominant zone, accounting for 48 per cent of launches (33,900 units) and 50 per cent of total sales (32,900 units). North Bengaluru showed the strongest year-on-year launch growth at 56 per cent. In budget terms, the high-end segment (INR 80 lakh–1.5 crore) captured the largest launch share at 39 per cent.
source: ANAROCK Indian Residential Real Estate Annual Report 2024
topic_tags: residential, launches, sales, 2024, East Bengaluru, North Bengaluru, ANAROCK

question: What was Bengaluru's office net absorption in 2024 and how did it rank among Indian cities?
answer: Bengaluru recorded 14.74 MSF of net office leasing activity in 2024 according to JLL, a 66 per cent increase from 9.01 MSF in 2023, making it the highest-ever annual absorption for the city. Cushman & Wakefield's data placed Bengaluru's net absorption at 14.18 MSF (28 per cent of India's total). Bengaluru led all Indian cities, followed by Mumbai at 10.93 MSF and Hyderabad at 8.18 MSF (CW data). India's total gross leasing volume across top-7 cities was 77.22 MSF in 2024 (JLL), while the top-8 city GLV was 89 MSF (CW) — both highest ever.
source: JLL Press Release 3 January 2025; Cushman & Wakefield Press Release 22 January 2025
topic_tags: commercial, office, absorption, 2024, record, Bengaluru, JLL, Cushman

question: How large were GCC and flex workspace segments in India's office market in 2024?
answer: Global Capability Centres captured 35.9 per cent of India's total office leasing market in 2024, leasing approximately 28 MSF across the top-7 cities (JLL). GCC leasing grew 41 per cent year-on-year to 25.7 MSF across the top-6 cities (Colliers). The flex office segment surged 50 per cent year-on-year, with leasing crossing 15 MSF in 2024 — accounting for 19.8 per cent of total annual leasing, its highest-ever share (JLL). Bengaluru and Hyderabad were the preferred GCC hubs, with US-headquartered technology and BFSI firms driving approximately 70 per cent of GCC demand.
source: JLL 2024 annual; Colliers 2025 GCC forecast; IBEF GCC report
topic_tags: GCC, flex, office, 2024, India, Bengaluru, JLL, Colliers

question: What did Knight Frank's H1 2025 data reveal about Bengaluru's residential launches and sales?
answer: Knight Frank's India Real Estate H1 2025 report (published July 2025) showed Bengaluru recorded 33,498 residential unit launches in the first half of 2025, the highest in recent history and 31 per cent above H1 2024 — the strongest city-wise launch growth in India. Bengaluru contributed 19 per cent of all-India new residential launches, behind only Mumbai (25 per cent). However, overall sales saw a modest 3 per cent year-on-year dip to 26,599 units in H1 2025. Homes priced INR 1-2 crore accounted for 47 per cent of total sales, with the INR 2-5 crore bracket at 24 per cent — together representing 79 per cent of all sales.
source: Knight Frank India Real Estate H1 2025 (cited in booknewproperty.com article 4 July 2025); GlobalPropertyGuide
topic_tags: residential, launches, sales, H1 2025, Knight Frank, premium, segmentation

question: What were Bengaluru's residential unit sales in Q3 2025 and the January–September 2025 cumulative?
answer: PropEquity estimated Bengaluru's residential sales at 16,840 units in Q3 2025 (July–September), a 21 per cent year-on-year increase from 13,966 units in Q3 2024. Cumulatively, sales for January–September 2025 were estimated at 49,559 units versus 46,392 units in the same period of 2024, representing a year-on-year gain of approximately 7 per cent. Developers were optimistic about a festive-season Q4 2025 uptick. India's top-nine cities were however expected to see a 4 per cent aggregate decline in Q3 2025 sales, primarily due to weaker markets in Mumbai and Pune.
source: PropEquity cited in Business Standard 28 September 2025
topic_tags: residential, sales, Q3 2025, PropEquity, January-September 2025

question: What has been the five-year residential price appreciation in Bengaluru from 2020 to 2025?
answer: Bengaluru residential property prices rose from approximately INR 4,700 per square foot in Q2 2020 to INR 8,720 per square foot in Q2 2025 — an appreciation of approximately 79-85 per cent over five years, according to Casagrand and NoBroker data. This makes Bengaluru the top-appreciating residential market among India's major metropolitan cities over this period, surpassing Delhi, Mumbai and Kolkata. ANAROCK's Q3 2025 data placed Bengaluru's average price at INR 8,870 per sq ft, representing 10 per cent year-on-year growth. Knight Frank's 2025 data showed Bengaluru leading all cities at 14 per cent annual price appreciation.
source: Casagrand blog Nov 2025; ANAROCK Q3 2025; Knight Frank H1 2025; GlobalPropertyGuide 2025
topic_tags: pricing, appreciation, 2020-2025, 5-year, INR, Casagrand

question: What did Cushman & Wakefield report about India's office market and Bengaluru in 2025?
answer: Cushman & Wakefield's January 2026 report on the India office market stated that India recorded a historic net absorption of 61 MSF in 2025, up 25 per cent year-on-year, making it the highest-ever annual net absorption for the country. Bengaluru contributed the largest share of this national total. In Q4 2025 specifically, Bengaluru recorded a gross leasing volume of approximately 7.6 MSF — the second-highest quarterly performance ever for the city. Bengaluru's Grade-A occupancy stood at approximately 90.8 per cent as of September 2025 per JLL/ICRA. ICRA forecast Bengaluru's net absorption at 16-16.5 MSF in FY2026.
source: Cushman & Wakefield MarketBeat January 2026; JLL; ICRA; bangaloreproperties.com December 2025
topic_tags: commercial, office, 2025, Cushman & Wakefield, 61 MSF, India record, Q4 GLV

question: How have Bengaluru residential rental trends evolved from 2023 to 2025?
answer: After witnessing a strong surge of 20-30 per cent in residential rentals during 2023-24, Bengaluru's rental market experienced a stabilisation phase from 2025. NoBroker reported that average rental inflation across Bengaluru in H1 2025 was 7-9 per cent, a significant moderation from the prior cycle, driven by increased housing supply and improving infrastructure creating more balanced demand-supply dynamics. However, North Bengaluru micro-markets including Devanahalli, Bagalur and Nelamangala continued to command 20-25 per cent expected rental appreciation due to proximity to employment hubs and major commercial setups. Kanakapura Road rental ceilings reached INR 35,000 per month post-Yellow Line commissioning.
source: NoBroker (cited in Economic Times); Hindustan Times Real Estate Outlook 2025; NoBroker 2026 article
topic_tags: rental, 2023-2025, NoBroker, moderation, North Bengaluru, Kanakapura Road

question: What is the commercial office outlook for Bengaluru in 2026 according to ICRA?
answer: ICRA estimates Bengaluru's Grade-A office stock at approximately 263 MSF — roughly 27 per cent of India's total across the top-six markets. For FY2026 (April 2025-March 2026), ICRA forecasts net absorption of 16-16.5 MSF for Bengaluru with occupancy expected to rise to 89-89.5 per cent by March 2026. Average office rentals are projected to grow 3-4 per cent annually in FY2025 and FY2026, with JLL projecting CBD rents growing at 6-7 per cent per year and strong increases in Whitefield and ORR micro-markets. Vacancy is expected to stay in single digits through FY2027, supported by GCC expansion. National office absorption projections for 2026 are 60-70 MSF across top cities.
source: ICRA; JLL; bangaloreproperties.com December 2025
topic_tags: commercial, office, 2026, ICRA, Grade-A, vacancy, rental growth

question: How did COVID-19 affect Bengaluru's delayed residential projects under RERA?
answer: COVID-19 had a significant impact on real estate project delivery timelines in Bengaluru. During the first wave in 2020, RERA Karnataka recorded approximately 20 delayed projects. By mid-2021 following the second wave, this number escalated sharply to 60 delayed projects involving approximately 29,850 units worth over INR 22,276 crore according to ANAROCK. Construction labour migration, material supply disruption and liquidity constraints were the primary causes. RERA Karnataka subsequently launched dedicated enforcement drives including a special cell for builder dues recovery, which contributed to a 30 per cent decline in new complaints by Q4 2023.
source: ANAROCK; RERA Karnataka; NoBroker 2026 article (special cell for dues recovery)
topic_tags: RERA, delayed projects, COVID, 2020-2021, enforcement, compliance

question: How has the budget segmentation of Bengaluru residential demand changed from 2020 to 2025?
answer: The budget mix of Bengaluru's residential market has shifted dramatically over 2020-2025. In 2020, over 87 per cent of new supply was in the sub-INR 80 lakh category (ANAROCK Q1 2020 data). By 2024, the high-end segment (INR 80 lakh–1.5 crore) captured the largest share at 39 per cent of new launches, followed by luxury (INR 1.5-2.5 crore) at 24 per cent; the mid-end at 27 per cent; and the affordable segment at just 8 per cent (ANAROCK 2024 Annual Report). By H1 2025, homes priced INR 1-5 crore together represented 79 per cent of all Bengaluru residential sales (Knight Frank H1 2025). In Q2 2025, luxury dominated new supply at 56 per cent of launches with zero affordable launches.
source: ANAROCK Q1 2020; ANAROCK Annual Report 2024; Knight Frank H1 2025; ANAROCK Q2 2025
topic_tags: segmentation, affordable, luxury, premium, 2020-2025, market shift

question: What metro infrastructure was built in Bengaluru between 2020 and 2026 and what property effect did each have?
answer: Three major metro milestones shaped Bengaluru's property market between 2020-2026. First, the Namma Metro Purple Line extension to Whitefield was completed in early 2023, triggering approximately 20 per cent year-on-year property appreciation in Whitefield and absorption of approximately 12,900 residential units in the corridor during 2023. Second, the 19-km Yellow Line from RV Road to Bommasandra via Electronic City became fully operational on 10-11 August 2024, driving 8-12 per cent appreciation along Electronic City and Kanakapura Road corridors; Kanakapura Road prices reached INR 7,000-14,000 per sq ft by 2025-26. Third, Metro Phase 3 — including a Hebbal-Airport HSR corridor and Bannerghatta extension targeting 314 km by 2030 — was approved in early 2026, generating forward appreciation sentiment in North Bengaluru and Bannerghatta Road.
source: BMRCL; NoBroker 2026 article; broker reports; PropSoch; Karnataka government approval
topic_tags: Namma Metro, Purple Line, Yellow Line, Phase 3, infrastructure, appreciation, BMRCL
